ai_strategy.summary: Lehigh has an unusually explicit public AI portal with draft guiding principles, a Generative AI Advisory Group report, LLM Gateway, Google/Gemini resources, career AI pathways, an AI tutor project, research tool access, and faculty/staff support. Its stance is mission-centered, human-centered, education-forward, and data-security aware.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: Lehigh University's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

bodies.name: Generative AI Advisory Group
bodies.scope: Explores how Lehigh can leverage AI to advance its mission and when purposeful non-use may be right.

bodies.name: Library and Technology Services
bodies.scope: Supports LLM Gateway, GPT Sandbox, seminars, consultations, and AI office hours.

governance_structure.summary: Lehigh University's public AI governance is organized through Generative AI Advisory Group, Library and Technology Services.

initiatives.title: Free Lehigh LLM Gateway for faculty and graduate students
initiatives.description: Lehigh LTS announced free faculty and graduate-student access to the LibreChat LLM Gateway with OpenAI, Perplexity, Claude, and Gemini in one interface, supplementing Google AI tools available to all campus users.
initiatives.significance: This closes a platform gap with a named multi-model campus gateway and audience scope.

investment.description: Free access is disclosed for faculty and graduate students, but contract or subsidy cost is not public.

collaborations.description: The gateway exposes multiple commercial AI model providers through a single Lehigh interface.

initiatives.policy_implications: Centralizes access and encourages data-classification review.

initiatives.title: Hawk and Sol GPU research computing capacity
initiatives.description: Lehigh’s LTS annual report discloses 229 GPUs and 27 million core hours delivered by its Hawk and Sol high-performance computing clusters in 2024-25.
initiatives.significance: This closes the compute-capacity gap with public GPU and usage metrics.

investment.description: No public total cost disclosed in the annual report.

collaborations.description: Hawk contributed core-hours to the Open Science Grid.

computing_infrastructure.summary: Lehigh LTS reports Hawk and Sol delivered 27 million core hours in 2024-25 on a shared platform with 172 nodes, 6,964 cores, 50 TB memory, 229 GPUs, and 4.2 TB GPU memory.

equity_and_access_initiatives.title: Guiding principles for AI at Lehigh
equity_and_access_initiatives.description: Draft principles emphasize access, reduced barriers, inclusion, human relationships, lawful use, privacy, fairness, and transparency.

regulatory_context.summary: Lehigh University operates in Pennsylvania; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and institutional privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: Lehigh University (Bethlehem, PA) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. Lehigh has an unusually explicit public AI portal with draft guiding principles, a Generative AI Advisory Group report, LLM Gateway, Google/Gemini resources, career AI pathways, an AI tutor project, research tool access, and faculty/staff support. Its stance is mission-centered, human-centered, education-forward, and data-security aware. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are Artificial Intelligence at Lehigh portal; Draft guiding principles for AI; Lehigh LLM Gateway and GPT Sandbox; Provost's AI Tutor Project; AI Ready career pathways. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
